methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $20M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Overview Energy, a Seed-stage space and aerospace company founded by Marc Berte and Andrew Cantino, has raised $20M to date. The broader Space & Aerospace sector comprises 6 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers, though the immediate peer set at identical stage and valuation tier is limited.

Of three triangulation methods examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the $20M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding an implied valuation of $50M. Peer-Median analysis could not be executed due to insufficient narrow-stage peers in Space & Aerospace (zero same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds). Public-Comp Anchoring was inapplicable because the target's revenue is not disclosed, precluding use of the sector's 42.9x median P/S multiple across the five public tickers.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $42M to $57M, with a median point of $50M. This range reflects modest upside from capital deployed but is grounded in a single methodology. Key uncertainties include the absence of stage-specific peer benchmarks in the Space & Aerospace sector and lack of revenue visibility, both of which limit triangulation depth. The valuation remains preliminary pending disclosure of financial metrics or identification of additional comparables at similar capital levels and development stages.
